commit_scope: Make introspection-led core lifting a goal, entry-flow prompt, and standing agent practice

commands: rg -n "North Star|Core lift|core-lift|Lift hot repetition|low-level recipes|hot-path|hot paths|repeated low-level" docs/PLAN.md AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md docs/shared/agent-start-packet.md scripts/prompt_entry_gate.sh
local_validation.summary: The plan now names the core-lift north star, shared agent instructions now treat hot repetition as a practice, the compact start packet carries the same rule for fresh agents, and prompt-guide prints the north-star reminder during entry. Wellness passed before the edit and prompt-guide passed after the edit with the new core-lift lines visible.

ci_validation.reason: No commit, push, or PR was created in this turn.

deploy_validation.reason: No production deploy was performed. This change updates repository guidance and local prompt-entry behavior.

phase_gate.reason: Local guidance proof passed, but CI and deployment validation remain pending until this work is committed and shipped.

evidence_refs: docs/PLAN.md adds a North Star section: introspection reveals repeated low-level recipes, especially hot paths, and lifts them into generic Form/BML teachings with proof.
AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md add the standing practice: route traces, JIT misses, carrier friction, static edges, and recipe clusters invite a reusable abstraction before local optimization.
docs/shared/agent-start-packet.md carries the same north star and prompt routing for fresh agents.
scripts/prompt_entry_gate.sh prints the core-lift reminder; make prompt-guide showed the new orientation and core-lift north-star output.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: New agents and follow-up turns encounter introspection-led core lifting as an explicit goal and entry-flow practice before adding or optimizing code.
test_flows: make prompt-guide prints the core-lift north-star entry lines.
bash -n scripts/prompt_entry_gate.sh validates the prompt-entry script syntax.
rg verifies the north-star language appears in the plan, agent instructions, start packet, and prompt-entry script.
